label: Apollo.io
description: Automate outbound prospecting and sequences using Apollo.io's contact database and engagement tools.

form.title: Connect Apollo.io
form.subtitle: Paste your Apollo.io API key to give Anton access to contacts, sequences, and enrichment.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste your Apollo.io API key. Found in Settings → Integrations → API. Quickest way to connect — no OAuth flow required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with an Apollo.io API Key

Apollo.io exposes a REST API secured by a personal API key tied to your account.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Apollo.io](https://app.apollo.io/).
2. Click your avatar or name in the bottom-left corner to open the account menu.
3. Go to **Settings → Integrations → API**.
4. You'll see a section labeled **API Key**. If no key exists yet, click **Generate API Key**.
5. Copy the displayed key.
6. Paste it into the **API Key** field below.

### Notes

- The API key grants access to your Apollo account at your user permission level — keep it secret.
- Apollo keys do not expire automatically, but you can regenerate them from the same page, which will invalidate the old key.
- If your organization uses team workspaces, make sure you're generating the key in the correct workspace.
- Read [Apollo's API documentation](https://apolloio.github.io/apollo-api-docs/) for full scope and rate-limit details.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in Apollo.io → Settings → Integrations → API. Keep this secret — it grants full API access at your permission level.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0
methods.description: Connect via OAuth 2.0 using your own Apollo OAuth application credentials. Best for multi-user setups or when you want token-based revocable access.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Apollo.io OAuth 2.0

OAuth 2.0 requires you to register an application in Apollo and bring your own client credentials.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Apollo.io](https://app.apollo.io/).
2. Go to **Settings → Integrations → API** and look for the **OAuth Applications** section.
3. Click **Create OAuth Application**.
4. Fill in the application name (e.g. `Anton`) and set the redirect URI to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect).
5. Copy the generated **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
6. Paste both into the fields below, then click **Submit** to open the Apollo authorization screen in your browser.
7. Approve access; Anton will store the resulting tokens automatically.

### Notes

- OAuth tokens can be revoked from Apollo's connected apps settings without affecting your API key.
- If Apollo's OAuth application feature is not available on your plan, use the **API Key** method instead.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Apollo.io → Settings → Integrations → API → OAuth Applications.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID from the same Apollo OAuth application.
